trade.summary: Modest bilateral trade. Brazil exports soybeans, sugar, chemicals, and aircraft to South Africa. South Africa exports platinum group metals, coal, vehicles, and machinery to Brazil.

diplomatic.summary: Brazil and South Africa are fellow BRICS members and coordinate on global governance reform, South-South cooperation, and development finance. The IBSA Dialogue Forum (India-Brazil-South Africa) is a significant multilateral mechanism. Both are leading developing economies with similar multilateral agendas.

military.summary: No military alliance. Some defense industry contacts. Both participate in UN peacekeeping. Growing cooperation in maritime security around the South Atlantic.

financial.summary: Limited but growing bilateral investment. Some Brazilian mining companies have South African connections through Anglo American and De Beers structures. New Development Bank (NDB) is a joint financial institution.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Some coal imports from South Africa. Cooperation on renewable energy and nuclear power technology.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in the IBSA framework. Joint research in tropical diseases, astronomy (SKA collaboration), and agriculture. Both countries share ecological challenges and climate vulnerabilities.
